english: not ...? i.e. o that! would that! ₒdi bdág-gi yín-na ći ma ruṅ ('di bdag gi yin na ci ma rung) would that this were mine! Thgy.; ṅai bú-mo mín-na ći ma ruṅ (ngai bu mo min na ci ma rung) I only wish, she were not my daughter! would it were not my daughter! Pth. b. ruṅ (rung) = yaṅ (yang) after a verbal root: de tsam żig bsdad ruṅ (de tsam zhig bsdad rung) though I have been sitting so long Mil.; mi dgos ruṅ (mi dgos rung) though it is not necessary Mil.; śes ruṅ mi śes-pa ltar byéd-pa (shes rung mi shes pa ltar byed pa) to plead ignorance although one knows the thing Mil.; ći-la ťug ruṅ (ci la thug rung) whatever may happen to me, = at all events, at any rate; ći yin ruṅ (ci yin rung) whatever it may be Mil.; log yin ruṅ min ruṅ (log yin rung min rung) whether it be an erroneous (opinion) or not Mil.; śi ruṅ gson ruṅ (shi rung gson rung) whether I live or die, living or dead Pth.; gaṅ yaṅ ruṅ (gang yang rung), ći yaṅ ruṅ (ci yang rung) whosoever he may be, whatsoever it may be, frq.; sa ču gaṅ yaṅ ruṅ-ba-la (sa chu gang yang rung ba la) on earth, water or whatever it be Do. c. mi-ruṅ-ba (mi rung ba) illicit, improper, unfit, v. above; mi-ruṅ-bai γżi bču (mi rung bai gzhi bcu) ten illicit actions, differently specified Tar. 33, 9, Köpp. I, 147, partly moral offences, partly only infractions of discipline; but ma-ruṅ-ba (ma rung ba), ma-ruṅs-pa (ma rungs pa) 1. pernicious, dangerous, atrocious, as enemies, beasts of prey, malignant gods and spirits, reckless destroyers etc. 2. spoiled, destroyed, ruined, ma-ruṅ-bar byéd-pa (ma rung bar byed pa) to destroy etc., ma-ruṅ-bar gyúr-ba (ma rung bar gyur ba) to be destroyed etc. Dzl.

a falling or fallen mass, as: k'a-rúd (kha rud) snow-slip, avalanche, ču-rúd (chu rud) deluge, inundation, flood (by the rupture of an embankment and the like), sa-rúd (sa rud) land-slide, descent of a great mass of earth; rúd-zam (rud zam) a snow-bridge, formed by avalanches.
prop. n., a district in the south of Ld.

to rush in upon, to attack, assault, p'yag żabs kún-nas rub-rúb byás-te (phyag zhabs kun nas rub rub byas te) rushing in upon him from every side in order to touch his hands and feet Mil.; bzán-la rúb-pa (bzan la rub pa) to pounce on the prey, to fall upon the food Glr.; *do-rub táṅ-te sád-če* (*rdo rub btang te bsad ce*) W. to kill with stones, to stone; *čog-čig-la rúb-pa* (*cog cig la rub pa*) W. to press or crowd together towards one side; k'a-rúb byéd-pa (kha rub byed pa) to outcry, to bear down by a louder crying Mil.; *go-rub-rúb ₒdug* (*mgo rub rub 'dug*) C., *go-rúb taṅ dug* (*mgo rub btang 'dug*) W. they put their heads together; *środ rub soṅ* (*srod rub song*), or mún-ču rub soṅ (mun chu rub song) W. darkness draws on, night is setting in, for which in C. *sa rub soṅ* (*sa rub song*) is said to be used, so that it might also be translated by to darken, to obscure.

1. womb, uterus, = mṅal (mngal), but less frq.: rum mi bde-ba (rum mi bde ba) sensations of pain during pregnancy Dzl., rúm-du ₒjúg-pa (rum du 'jug pa) to enter into the womb. — 2. darkness, obscurity, mún-pai rum (mun pai rum) Glr., gen. smag-rúm (smag rum). — 3. prop. n., Turkey, the Ottoman empire, the site of which is but vaguely known to the Tibetans, though some commodities from thence find their way to Lhasa; rúm-pa (rum pa) a man from Turkey, a Turk; rum-śam (rum sham) (شام) Syria Cs.

to rot, to get rotten, to become putrid, to turn rancid etc., rúl-bar gyúr-ba (rul bar gyur ba) B., *rul čá-če* (*rul ca ce*) W. id.; ₒo-ma rul soṅ ('o ma rul song) the milk is spoiled, k'a rul (kha rul) the snow does no longer bear, *be rul* (*bye rul*) W. drift-sand, quicksand; rul-skyúr (rul skyur) 'sour by putrefaction' Sch; rúl-dri (rul dri) a putrid smell; rúl-po (rul po) for hrúl-po (hrul po) Cs. — Cf. ₒdrul-ba ('drul ba).

1., W. rus-pa (rus pa), lineage, family, miṅ daṅ rus ni ₒdi-ltar-ro (ming dang rus ni 'di ltar ro) their name and lineage are such and such Glr.; *ṅa-raṅ-ghi* (*nga rang gi*) (or ṅa-raṅ dhaṅ (nga rang dang)) rụ-čig-pa (ru cig pa) or -ḍá-wa* (-da wa*) C.B., *rús-pa čig-čig* (*rus pa cig cig*) W. we are of the same family; rus-γčíg-pa gsód-pa (rus gcig pa gsod pa) a murderer of persons related to him by blood Lex.; ťu-mi rus (thu mi rus) Lex.: Thu-mi, a family-name; rus mtó-ba (rus mtho ba) high extraction, rus dmá-ba (rus dma' ba) low extraction Cs. — 2. v. the next article.

(resp. gduṅ (gdung)) 1. bone, rus-čág (rus chag) fracture of a bone Med.; rús-pai dúm-bu (rus pai dum bu) prob. small bones of which the Tibetan anatomy enumerates 360. — mí-ru (mi ru) human bone; rkáṅ-rus (rkang rus) bone of the foot; mgó-rus (mgo rus) bone of the skull; rús-pai rgyan (rus pai rgyan) Mil. a decoration of terrifying deities and magicians, consisting of human bones suspended from the girdle; rús-pai rgyan drug (rus pai rgyan drug) Pth., the like ornament, but fastened to six different parts of the body, the top of the
